release_increment_rules.model_change: increment segment 1, reset segments 2, 3, 4 to zero
release_increment_rules.service_change: increment segment 2, reset segments 3, 4 to zero
release_increment_rules.bug_fix: increment segment 3, reset segment 4 to zero
release_increment_rules.multiple_changes: highest-order change wins, all lower segments reset to zero

file_versioning.V0_convention: no version folder means V0 is implied

file_versioning_rules.additive_only: new versions are created by adding new files, not overwriting
file_versioning_rules.folder_structure: Vn folders are subfolders of the original location
file_versioning_rules.exception_alignment: versioned exceptions live beneath the corresponding versioned model path
file_versioning_rules.earlier_versions: earlier-version code remains available unless explicitly deprecated/removed

api_versioning_rules.behavior_version_explicit: api/Vn.m/{resource} denotes model version n with behavior version m
api_versioning_rules.route_alignment: API route must align with underlying model/service pair versioning

deprecation.consumer_notice_requirement: consumers MUST be given enough time to act before sunset

capabilities.purpose: discovery of supported features before invocation

operating_principles: follow explicit versioning rules exactly as defined
do not replace with inferred semantic versioning practices
prefer additive versioned structure over destructive replacement
surface capabilities where version differences matter to consumers
forbidden: hiding model changes inside service segment
hiding service changes inside bug/config segment
using build increment to represent code change
overwriting earlier versions instead of creating new versioned files
weakening storage constraints at foundation layer
sunsetting APIs without adequate consumer notice period
removing earlier versions without explicit deprecation strategy
